page: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-8xCPU-128GB is a memory-optimized virtual server built on the KVM hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an AMD EPYC 7542 processor running at 2.0 GHz. The configuration provides 8 vCPUs mapped to 8 physical cores with a shared CPU allocation model. It is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high resource density of 16.0 GB of RAM per core. No local storage or dedicated GPUs are bundled with this instance.

Performance benchmarks indicate a clear distinction between single-core and multi-core capabilities. Single-core CPU performance under stress-ng is positioned in the bottom 10%, reflecting the shared CPU allocation and modest 2.0 GHz clock speed. However, multi-core CPU performance, Geekbench multi-core scores, and PassMark CPU metrics consistently rank in the middle 50%. Memory bandwidth tests for both small and large blocks also fall within average parameters, though the PassMark memory score is weaker, placing in the bottom 25%.

The server demonstrates average throughput for standard workloads, including Redis SET operations, static web serving, and software compilation. For specialized tasks, it handles small-scale language model inference at average speeds, but performance drops significantly to the bottom 10% for medium 7B models. Given the high memory capacity relative to the shared CPU resources, this server offers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-intensive workloads that do not require sustained, high-performance single-core compute. It is well-suited for large-scale caching, in-memory databases, and general-purpose backend applications with high RAM requirements.
description: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-8xCPU-128GB is a memory-optimized virtual server utilizing the KVM hypervisor and an x86_64 AMD EPYC 7542 processor. It features 8 vCPUs with shared CPU allocation and a substantial 128.0 GB of RAM, providing 16.0 GB of memory per core. The instance does not include local storage or GPU resources. Benchmark data shows average multi-core CPU performance and memory bandwidth, contrasting with poor single-core performance in the bottom 10% due to its shared CPU model. This resource profile makes the server a cost-efficient option for memory-heavy workloads that do not demand continuous, high-frequency single-core execution. It is best suited for in-memory databases, caching layers, and memory-intensive backend applications.
og_description: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-8xCPU-128GB is a memory-optimized KVM server featuring 8 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs and 128 GB of RAM, ideal for memory-intensive workloads requiring moderate multi-core performance.

tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server featuring a high RAM-to-core ratio and shared AMD EPYC processors for memory-intensive workloads.

High memory density of 16 GB RAM per physical core
